To my friend who ghosted me randomly, I was thinking about what to tell you— what I would say if I had the chance. You’ll more than likely never see this kasi bihira ka lang tumingin dito sa freedom wall, kung hindi ko pa i-forward [REDACTED_NAME] viral posts hindi mo pa ma-ge-gets yung reference [REDACTED_NAME]. But silence like this leaves a weight behind. And this is my way of setting it down rather than being crushed by the weight of it all. You made me feel like I mattered to you. So I trusted you. I let myself believe we were building a foundation for a lifetime of friendship, [REDACTED_NAME] maging friends [REDACTED_NAME] nga tayo kaya dapat sila rin maging magkasundo. We shared things about ourselves we barely even remember before we met, you said so yourself that you remembered some things about you because of me. That stayed with me. It meant something. It opens a door to deeper honesty. But instead of walking through it, you disappeared. Not just pulled back, but blocked me entirely. I know you don't owe me anything naman kasi lahat naman [REDACTED_NAME] out of my own free will. But I hope that you change, eventually. I know you are not a bad person. I know that if given the choice, you prefer becoming the better version of yourself every time. So, I hope you change for the better. Do not do it again to someone else. Don't ghost anyone else. [REDACTED_NAME] mo. [REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME]. I don’t hate you. I just won’t pretend this didn’t happen. Goodbye. I hope you learn to stay, eventually.

A mini guide for incoming freshies This is a list of pages I find useful for my everyday Elbi Life * [CALABARZON] Student Help desk- mostly for questions from currently enrolled students. (Incoming freshies mag search button at Google maps [REDACTED_NAME] before posting here [REDACTED_NAME].) *[CALABARZON] Freedom Wall- most appropriate alternative place if gusto niyo magtanong ng what to expect IMO, para hindi flooded [REDACTED_NAME] not so urgent questions niyo. *[CALABARZON] Housemates- dorm recos, reviews etc. Mostly for non-UP owned dorms. You can also ask for transients here etc. *[CALABARZON] Office of Student Housing- If you want to apply/inquire for dorms INSIDE [CALABARZON] and UP owned *[CALABARZON] - yung verified page, for official announcements *UP Shiftees and Transferees - how to shift, how to transfer etc. Pages to follow na mostly when you are current student [REDACTED_NAME] helpful *[CALABARZON] DX AMIS Support Group (enrollment purposes) *[CALABARZON] University Library (study place + verification of ID) *Respective student council pages *[REDACTED_NAME] and Found I probably missed a lot of other helpful pages that you may learn about from your ates and kuyas :) Some important reminders 1.Search Function exists 2.Google Maps exists 3.Follow the assigned schedule especially during enrollment season tapos magchecheck pa kayo ng class schedule niyo by accessing AMIS kahit di niyo pa naman maview. 4.Wearing ID Laces are completely normal 5.Pakiiwan Main Character Syndrome(if [REDACTED_NAME]) niyo sa bahay niyo. Remember to be responsible and respectful adults befitting someone na pinapaaral [REDACTED_NAME] Congrats and Welcome to ELBI !

I feel like a terrible person, but I chose to put myself first. Hi. I’ve never had a boyfriend before (NBSB). Recently, I met someone online. He seemed nice, and he said things I had never really heard before—like he was willing to risk everything for me. I think I was craving love and attention for a long time, so I quickly said yes. But what started as something comforting slowly felt overwhelming. He began talking about serious future plans very quickly, like asking my parents to let me study in his country and live there when he comes to the Philippines. I told him multiple times that I wasn’t comfortable with that. I want to build my own future and make my own decisions without rushing into something so big. I don’t think I’m ready for that kind of step. I genuinely wish him well.

[REDACTED_NAME], 4 years. ([REDACTED_NAME]?! dinaig 7 years?!) i loved you for four years, even though we were never really anything. kahit once a year lang tayo nagkakausap, and right after we meet, you disappear again like nothing ever happened. it was stupid of me to believe there could be something real between us. the last time we saw each other, it was the first time you held my hand, hugged me, and kissed me. and somehow, i already knew that was going to be the last. tanda [REDACTED_NAME] magkasama tayo, at sinabi mo sa’kin na hindi mo nakikita sarili mo na magkakaroon [REDACTED_NAME]. doon pa lang, [REDACTED_NAME]. i just chose to hope anyway (noon). [REDACTED_NAME] not because i still want you, but because you left me confused and disrespected, like i was something you could pick up and drop whenever it was convenient for you. every time i see you, sumasakit [REDACTED_NAME]—not out of love, but because i remember how easily you made me feel abandoned. i don’t want you anymore. i’m finally happy with my life, and i mean that. pero nandito pa rin yung galit at sakit, not because i miss you, but because i know i didn’t deserve the way you treated me. but still, thank you. you made me realize that i can love that long, that deeply. and if i was capable of loving the wrong person for four years, then i know i’ll be capable of loving the right one even better. maybe it’s my fault too. i was never fully authentic with you. i tried so hard to understand you, to like everything you liked, that i slowly lost the chance to show you who i really was. i wanted closure before, but now i see it for what it is. your inconsistency was the closure. your silence was the answer. you not choosing me, over and over again, was everything i needed to know. i don’t need an apology anymore. whether you realize you hurt me or not, that’s on you. i wish you the best. do well in philosophy. and maybe this is the curse i gave myself—saying i’d spend my whole life getting over you. but the truth is, i am over you. just not over the pain. - carson, hindi pa nakakagraduate sa sakit. To the most attractive person in our department, Hi. I’m not sure if you’ve noticed, but I really like you. There are moments when I catch myself looking your way a little longer than I should and it's hard not to. Your smile has a way of making even the most ordinary day feel lighter. I think about you more often than I’d like to admit, and somehow you always come up in my thoughts. Hi. I’m a young adult seeking advice on relationships. I always thought that love was a big paradox because it was so simple and yet so complex at the same time. But now that I’m getting older, I’ve come to realize why, that love as a concept is a two-way string: love can be simple in its existence as a feeling, but it is rarely ever allowed to be transmuted into action, whether that be because of self-built ceilings or external circumstances. To cut it short, I think I ‘love’ someone who I’m slowly starting to realize doesn’t exactly fit the life I’m building towards right now, and they’re not exactly showing that they’re aware of this gap either. How do you deal with that? Do you just let them go? How do you learn to compromise? Should I, as a young woman who has her eyes set on her own goals, really have to be the one to go out of my way to ask for it? Is it a sign it’s not for me entirely if I have to mold it into something else? How do you let go of potential when it’s as formless as air but it feels so whole in the palm of your hand?

Hey so, I know this might be an uncommon knowledge (because I've also had to deal with this from a lot of people) but just like how we were taught to always flush the toilet after use (although a lot of y'all still seem to not practice by default), let's also make it a habit to flush the bathroom floor after showering. I sure do hope cleaning out the drain of hair and gunk after the shower is also already a common knowledge, but i also wouldn't want other people, let alone my room/house mates to have to deal with residues of my menses or volvolisms that were left on the floor after showering. If you understand how shameful it would be if you left the toilet unflushed after your use (especially if everyone knew you were the last to use it) then I hope you'd also be able to understand how shameful leaving the bathroom floor with your "wash-offs" would be.

What I don't understand is that I still get feelings for other people. Sometimes I think I have "crushes" on classmates, people I barely know. It's not as intense as how I feel for my partner (obviously), but it still makes me feel like I'm betraying them. (Maybe I am.) In response to While the intention of this post is to raise awareness, it can be a bit risky, especially since a photo is included. Based on the timeline of the post, the test was done around two weeks after exposure using a typical rapid test kit. However, these kits usually take about a month (or more) from exposure or contact before antibodies become detectable. This means it’s also possible that the infection (if confirmed through further laboratory testing) may have come from an earlier exposure. Let this also serve as a reminder, if you are not fully confident with your partner, always practice safe s*x, get tested regularly, and consider enrolling in PrEP.

It hit me late last night, somewhere between the third and fourth page of my essay on feminism. I was writing about autonomy and the room of one’s own, and I realized I had been building mine for years. Listen, I don't want princess treatment, I want dream girl treatment. I don’t want to be a princess locked in a tower of high expectations; I want to be part of the life you dream of. I am capable, whole, and I’ve learned how to turn my own solitude into a sanctuary. I don’t need a savior to carry my bags or clear my path. I need a partner who sees the fire I’ve built and wants to keep it bright. Show me that I am the answer to a prayer you whispered into the dark. Ask for my perspective, value my voice, and remember that my autonomy isn’t a threat to your manhood, it’s my strength. I can be a girly girl and then switch into a strong woman in a split second. Love me not by doing things for me, but by growing beside me. Respect the woman I am today, and be the loudest voice cheering for the woman I’m becoming. I want you to tell me that I am you dream girl, not your princess.

I've never written sa freedom [REDACTED_NAME] gusto ko lang mag vent. Ang hirap mag move on from unclear endings. It was hard for me to move on, from when we last spoke in [REDACTED_NAME] in July all the way to September. I spent sleepless nights regretful of my actions and timing. I prayed every day that we could fix things. [REDACTED_NAME] I started it; there is no moment where I see a glimpse of you that I don't remember my mistake. Everytime we would meet, by sheer chance--like that one time we bumped into each other on the street--I could never tell if you were resenting me or longing to talk to me again. I would ask how you're doing, but your answers felt cold. I could never tell what you were thinking or how you felt. It was all so muddy and so unclear. I tried to make amends. I tried to clear things. I gave you space. I wanted to comfort you. I wanted to mourn with you. But what else was I supposed to do when time passes by? When things are unclear? When things were left to a "maybe"? Just keep waiting? How long? It seems like you didn't want to talk--even at the very least, clarify things. Is making the effort to communicate a one-way thing? I understand you were going through hell and that giving me a clear yes or no was the least of your priorities. But how long was I to wait? Am I wrong for realizing that no answer is an answer? It was September when I realized that there's no hearing from you. I decided to move on. I thought your silence meant you were telling me off. Is it wrong for me to move on? Is it wrong for me to find love, when I had moved on? I resolved in my heart to move on completely before pursuing anyone. I waited 4 months since September to make sure I had completely moved on. I hurts to see your friends giving me looks, [REDACTED_NAME]. Pero walang clarity eh and now we're both left guessing what either of us are up to. I h*te that I am probably the subject of gossip. I just wish we clarified things. I just wish we could have been more communicative. I'm not blaming you. I understand you're still mourning, busy, and on your last semester. I'm just sad that things had to be this way and had to be this way. I also shouldn't blame you because the absolute shit-fest of events that happened in your life all at once could break anyone. And I'm sorry for adding to it. I'm doing better and making efforts to be more sensitive. If by some sheer chance you know who this is, I just want to tell you, I'm grateful to have met you--I'm grateful to have known you. I admire your courage and your strength. I pray for you and your family when I can. I'm thankful for your gifts. You're thoughtful, beautiful, funny, [REDACTED_NAME], and full of joy; you touch the lives of the people you meet, in ways you don't realize. At least, that's what I caught in our short moment of dating. I hope the next man you meet will discover the beauty of you, make it flourish. I'm sorry for my timing. But I also cannot deny that communication is so important, and that to work through hard times is the most loving thing we can do. I genuinely believe that we could have been compatible, but I guess it just isn't now. To quote, from [REDACTED_NAME], "compatability is not the precondition of love, but compatability is the fruit of love." Perhaps our idea of love was not the same. Maybe in another lifetime. Anyway. That's it. I wish you the best

i peaked in high school lmao (I KNOW THIS POST WILL BE BORING SO THERE'S A TL;DR AT THE END) never thought i'd say this, but i fell off. i graduated SHS with 10 medals. i won an individual award during the DSPC as a first-timer, qualified for regionals, won a research competition with another individual award, and won another individual award for a separate research project. my adviser took 30 seconds to finish reading my accolades when it was finally my turn to walk up to the stage. needless to say, i was ecstatic. that day, i felt a sense of euphoria that i hadn't felt before. it's as if i had finally realized my potential; the same potential that my family had been pointing out for years. i felt complete. then, 3 months later, UP comes around and kicks me right in the b*lls. i spent my first semester here trying not to make a fool out of myself. in the end, i got what i wanted: a GWA of 1.4. my family continued to praise my results as a university scholar, but i don't even think it counts. i had to drop a course to save myself. i never thought i'd see myself having to resort to a crutch like course-dropping just to get by. now that i'm in my second semester, my scores are suffering even more. idk what to atp lol tl;dr and moral of the story: try not to use accomplishments as your sole benchmark for happiness. take it from me, ts made me miserable

Dated someone for a long time and things ended cleanly. No sh*uting, no f*ghting, no resentment, no h*te. Only love and understanding that our paths just crossed. This s*cks more than a messy breakup. Grieving is harder, if I may say. Although I already grieved for you before this day came. One might say we didn't fight hard enough... we did, but something for some reason was eating the other alive. I just pray and hope I was able to show give you the love you earned and know that you deserve it... and that you are allowed to receive it. I may not have been the best at showing up, but I hope you know that I always tried to find words to say how much I love you (as a sucker for words of affirmation). I can still recall clearly you opening your dorm room inviting me in, remembering this makes me yearn so freaking hard as only you made me realize that I can be loved, that I am welcome. Maybe soon, once I am in a better space and you are able to hold the love I offer, if that happens, I would be the most blessed person alive. Sorry is an understatement for things I have caused you. In a month, year or so when that ever haplens, I hope you open the door for me for a brief conversation as I want to ask forgiveness for a very specific moment in your life you wished for me to be there and I wasn't. For now, enjoy the journey, keep your head high, and reach for your dreams. I love you so so much pork belly.

Roommates, just a gentle reminder to please be considerate during nighttime at 7 PM onwards especially when others are already sleeping. The constant mouse clicking and loud alarms can be really disruptive—it causes headaches and affects sleep, which in turn impacts performance in class the next day especially the rest of the week. I can't have a restful weekend because of that. If possible, let’s try to minimize noise during sleeping hours—use quieter options like a trackpad, lower alarm volumes, or step outside if needed or study outside the room if needed. Upper batches na tayo, and most of us are preparing for internship and thesis, so rest is really important. Let’s all be mindful of each other’s space and well-being. Thank you.

Just a late-night realization on what it actually feels like to be chosen by a real man. Ladies, save yourself for someone who really cares, stop settling for "digital affection." We’ve normalized guys who think a heart reaction to an IG story is the peak of romance, but real intimacy isn't found in a notification. It’s found in the person who is actually there to take the picture of you and for you. What separates my current relationship from the ones that left me drained is one simple thing: Consistency. He doesn't just show up for the main events; he’s there for the quiet, unglamorous ones, too. When I had my thesis proposal defense that I thought I was unprepared for, he didn't just send a "good luck" text. He traveled hours from his hometown because he refused to let me face this daunting moment alone. And when I passed a random quiz I’d been stressing over? He was just as ready to celebrate that tiny win as if I’d won an [REDACTED_NAME]. He would even being treats like ice cream and chocolates for me. The most romantic part? He stayed when there was nothing in it for him. Even when I told him I wasn't ready for a relationship, he remained a steady, quiet presence—not to "win" me over, but because he genuinely cared as a friend first and foremost, before a romantic partner. I realized real love doesn't feel like a performance to win points over you. It feels safe. It’s the difference between being remembered when it’s convenient and being chosen every day. Wait for the man who doesn't just double-tap your life from a screen, but actually shows up to live it with you.

This is a response to . Why ghosting is actually a healthy and normal thing. A more mature way of approach is to accept that not all people are capable of saying goodbye when they feel they don't want to take part in a communication anymore. Not all are capable of processing this emotion and the necessary actions of letting the other party know what is happening. Ghosting is acceptable, normal, healthy for people to do. You can ghost your friends if they are no longer in line with your goals and lifestyle. You can ghost anyone for your peace of mind. 1. Communication on the modern day has gotten on a really different level. It is so fast and the values of people have changed alongside it. Being "seen" is considered somehow an insult. Being "left on read" is a malign. The quick and easy way of responding to messages has gotten most of us in expecting the fastest replies. Gone are the days of snail mail waiting months for a prized message from someone important. 2. It is not surprising that we see new villains the people who ghost. We see them as cruel, insensitive, and why not? They left us hanging with no explanations. Where did we go wrong? Did we become uninteresting? Why can't they just say goodbye? We think a word would satisfy us. But the truth is, ghosting is something that is natural. Just because sending a message is easy doesn't mean it is easy to construct a meaningful one. 3. In a real life communication, not everything we say to someone will be greeted with enthusiasm. Sometimes, they could just gesture a simple nod or a grunt. If they have something to contribute, they will say it and if you are lucky, it would lead to a truly interesting conversation. Now, imagine this in the virtual world. It is cruel to always expect an equally enthusiastic reply from someone. We should accept that truly engaging talks are rare to come by and should be treasured. 4. Expecting someone to keep up conversations with us is putting them in our own projections. We project that we text them, they would reply, we enjoy a fun night talking to each other. The truth is not everyone is capable of doing this. Problems arise, people become sad, people need time and people need to ignore messages. It is then asking too much to expect someone to always and constantly attend to our needs in the virtual plane of communication. 5. Now, let us say that none of the things mentioned were the cause of ignoring or ghosting. What if the other person's interest towards us just fizzled out? Should we blame them for chickening out to say "I am sorry, I believe I am not interested in keeping our communication going." It is not an easy thing to say and definitely something that someone should not be obliged to say. It is our task to accept that we are not always interesting, or that people we talk to don't have fountain sources of replies. We should accept that fizzling out is a characteristic of any conversation.

Giving my perspective as the friend who was ghosted before. I can tell you that letting go is something we must do again and again, feeling the sting anew each time. To those who have ghosted someone who once loved or cared for them, understand this: no one lets go in an instant. You might see your departure as a single, swift act, but for the one left behind, the reality is a slow, repetitive unraveling. It happens in the quiet moments when we instinctively reach for our phones to send you a photo of a cute stray cat or a funny stray thought, only to pull our hands back because while your ghost remains, you are no longer there. It happens when we pass a place we once visited or hear an echo of our old conversations. These are constant, tiny heartbreaks—a messy, lingering grief where we are forced to release you piece by piece, moment by moment. Please, do not leave us haunting the spaces you used to fill. Don’t ghost the people who care for you; just give us the truth, no matter how much it hurts.

I just want to share something as it was weighing on my mind for a couple of years now. There's this guy that nireto [REDACTED_NAME] that time, and syempre I stalked him on everything. I came to know that he is wealthy. At the time, I had a prejudice [REDACTED_NAME] like na they're masungit and that they wouldn't mingle at all sa mga katulad [REDACTED_NAME] or anything. [REDACTED_NAME] even before we talked, I already have this view of him na baka nga ganun siguro [REDACTED_NAME]. So you wouldn't believe how shocked I was when I learned that he was a very caring, kind and responsible person. He was everything that I could've wanted and more. He's very talented. He composes songs and sings it and even produces it all by himself. At first, it was everything to me. I loved his voice, I loved the way he shares reels of the most random of things, I love the way he greets me good morning and how he genuinely asks questions because he want to learn more about me. Looking back, I don't even know how he loved me when my replies are dry af. Then, he wrote me a song. It was the first time in my life that someone ever made me a song. It was so beautiful, that I listen to it now more times than I would care to admit. It was about him wanting to see me after the pandemic is over. Everything was perfect, but that was exactly why I was terrified. I was terrified that all of this were too good to be real and that one day he'd realize that I wasn't good enough. When he said I love you for the first time, I was both ecstatic and terrified. That none of it was real and once he gets to see me and interact with me for real, he'd leave me and I'd be all alone again. This was at the same time that classes started to shift into blended learning. After a few days, I got to meet someone I had a crush on before, and some of my feelings for him started to reappear. I talked with him, and interacted with him because we went to the same school. I talked less and less with the guy na nireto [REDACTED_NAME] friend [REDACTED_NAME] and eventually, I didn't text him anymore. Essentially, I ghosted him. I regret it. I regret it every day. A few months later, I entered a relationship with a new guy. He was sweet, charming, all the works. It didn't work out in the end and he eventually got back with his ex. After a few months, a new school year began and a lot of transferees came. One of them knew the guy na nireto sakin and I found out na he asked about me sa transferee if nandun [REDACTED_NAME] and if I was doing well. That crushed me. The thought that I left someone so genuinely kind and broke his heart. What hurt me more was the fact that after all those months, he still cared about me. I told the transferee to tell the guy na nakamove on nako and na I was in a relationship. I wasn't, but I wanted him to move on far from me, so that he wouldn't get hurt, but maybe I was just protecting myself from the consequences of my actions. Maybe I was protecting myself from thinking that I broke that guy's heart. So that I wouldn't be at fault. Diba? Apaka gag*? [REDACTED_NAME] man [REDACTED_NAME], or maybe [REDACTED_NAME] nagchat. Through mutual friends pa talaga. Recently, I think about him a lot. I listen to the song he dedicated for me. A lot. I wish him all the happiness in the world. I hope he forgets about me. I hope he h*tes me, because it would hurt me more to know that he isn't mad at me. He took a business major at a major university now. I know because I once asked him what he wanted to take for college. In the off-chance that you see this, I really hope you're happy where you are. I know it's shameless of me to say this after all these years, but, I loved you.

Most, if not all, major universities in Metro Manila have already suspended classes due to the nationwide transport strike. Meanwhile, in a public institution like [CALABARZON], where you’d expect a deeper understanding of students who rely on public transport; the administration passed the decision to individual Faculty-in-Charge. The same FICs who, conveniently, have their own vehicles. Unsurprisingly, not one of my four classes shifted online. Four back-to-back classes. Different buildings. No reliable transport. This isn’t just poor planning, it’s outright disregard. Let’s be clear: not all students have the luxury of private vehicles. Many of us depend entirely on public transportation just to get to class, let alone move between them. Calling this “inconsiderate” is putting it lightly. This is what a disconnect from student reality looks like.

Open letter to my former close friend. I don’t regret loving you, I regret how it ended. I wasn’t looking for love when things developed between us. But things really developed quickly. In less than a year, I already fell in love with you. I guess part of it is because it felt like if life can throw us this many curveballs and we are still together, then maybe you are the one. And I guess a part of me always wanted to show you what love truly is. I was there for you during the hardest time of your life, I felt that if the shoe were on the other foot, you’d do the same. I didn’t do that because I wanted a debt of gratitude, but due to us being friends. One random night while we were talking you asked me if I had romantic feelings for you, I never figured that it would be an issue that I fell for you. All I cared about was you. You said you may have commitment or abandonment issues or that you wondered if you’re a bad person. I guess I always tried to reassure that I would never leave you, that I was cocky enough to show you love where you wouldn’t fear commitment. Little did I know that showing more love would bring that fear more. Of course, I tried to play it cool but little did you know that I was so happy. I wanted to make you so happy that I admit there were times I treated you as more than a friend. So when things finally blew up in my face when you realized you don't want to be friends anymore and some boundaries not being maintained, my safety shattered. I didn’t want to fix you. I just wanted to be there for you, so that you can have the best conditions to fix yourself. I knew you were independent and that you can do them on your own. But instead, you were overwhelmed because of me. You said you can’t give me what I deserve or need in terms of romantic love. I was willing to endure but you were more willing to discard. Then you just became a ghost to me anyway. I don’t regret loving you, but I do regret how it ended. I hope you’re doing well.

Should I feel terrible about myself? There are many posts here about avoidant-type of people, and I realized that I'm one of those people. Usually the posts are about love and ghosting in relationships, but I have no interest in that, I identify as an aro-ace. But even then, I ghost and neglect to reply to my friends, well, I don't even know if I can call them that, its just- I overthink a lot and I find it difficult to reply to people through chat. I think its easier to interact face to face. But mostly, when people chat to me, its about acads, org work, or they have something they need from me. Even then, I still find it difficult to think of a reply to that kind of message. I don't even know how to apologize to them. I don't know if I should feel sad about it. The fact that I have no genuine friends. I'm still in my first year, I know I have many chances to make friends, but I find it difficult to maintain relationships. I have no idea how to be a friend. What do people even talk about when there is nothing that could connect them (like an event, or a group work)? Maybe its brought upon by myself, like I don't really even want friends, but nobody is like that. It sounds lonely. I'm a coward. I'm content with this. I'm fine with being by myself. I'm sorry.

I feel that this stuff is way harder than breaking up with a guy.

If we do not have our own large reserve of it, we will need to buy from other countries that will also up their selling price dahil nagkakaubusan ng gas/oil. To make up for this, tataas [REDACTED_NAME] prices [REDACTED_NAME] to have enough money to import the resource. [REDACTED_NAME] to function - to go to where we need and come back home. When we use transpo vehicles, they use gas/oil. It might be small if we're looking at daily commute lang and personal use sa private cars but all together, they stack up. We can not continue consuming resources at the same rate we normally do because there is scarcity ng resources na 'yon. Distribution/use must be limited if we want to last a bit longer, to survive a bit longer. Hence, limiting movement aids in conserving what little resources we have. Our current situation is not good - not ideal - but it is what we need to do for now given the context of wars/conflicts that is affecting our world. I get frustrated when people assume I don’t know how to be happy on my own. I’ve been alone for almost a decade. That’s a long time to sit with yourself. It hasn’t been the most exciting decade of my life, but after that long, you learn how to live with your own company. You get used to it. You make peace with it. So when people talk to me like I’ve never learned how to be alone, it feels off. I already know that life. I’ve been living it. Another thing that gets to me is when people say I need to “work on myself.” I get the idea behind it, but it often sounds like I’m somehow unfinished or not enough as I am. Whatever happened to just being your authentic self? The truth is, everyone is a work in progress for as long as they’re alive. No one wakes up one day and suddenly becomes a fully fixed person. What makes it harder is when the people saying this are also carrying their own damage and loneliness, but they speak like they’ve figured life out. They give advice like they’re standing on higher ground, when really we’re all just trying to get through our own mess. These conversations almost always circle back to the same thing. Love. Finding the right guy. And somehow it always lands on the idea that I must be the problem. Maybe I am. I’ve thought about that more times than I’d like to admit. Sometimes I even wonder if maybe I’m just not meant to be loved. Because when I look back at my past relationships, a pattern shows up. I’m either someone they run to when it’s convenient, or I become a kind of experiment. A place where they try to fix their own issues. And when they’re done, or when they figure themselves out, they leave. Just like that. So before anyone tells me to fix myself, I wish they knew this. I have tried. I’m still trying. Every single day. And somehow, no matter how much effort I put in, I still end up in the same place. Alone. And lonely.

i hope we are a little gentler, a little kinder. i hope that when people fail to meet deadlines or tasks, instead of lashing out, i hope we ask them: are you okay? i hope we have a little more empathy to struggles that hinder people from performing well, because often times, these struggles remain unseen and unheard. i hope that our pursuit of excellence does not erode our kindness, not just to others, but most importantly to ourselves. i hope that we do not forget the heart that would cradle our excellence. in a system where people are forced to the extremes for "productivity", let us strive for a culture of concern, compassion, and empathy. - marionette strings

I think I enjoy being hugged because it’s like something is keeping me together. Oftentimes I feel like I’m falling apart. Like something is splitting me down the center, and I have to try my hardest to hold myself together lest everything go badly again. I don’t feel whole. I rarely do. As if one side is in control over the other constantly. But when someone hugs me, It’s as though something is telling me “Hey, it’ll be alright. You’re okay.” And all of a sudden, I’m not a bad person trying to be better. I’m not a washed up gifted kid who lost his spark and is trying to bring it back. I’m just me. And “me” is okay.

I used to admire her quietly. She’s the kind of woman people notice right away; smart, beautiful, the kind of beauty people sometimes mistake for Korean that people assume she is way younger than her actual age. But what made her unforgettable to me was how brave and thoughtful she was. The way she carried herself, the way she cared deeply about things, the way she kept moving forward no matter what, the way she found courage within her when it matters. So I stayed in my place, supporting her the only way I knew how. [REDACTED_NAME], but always there. Cheering for her, believing in her, celebrating even the smallest things she accomplished. And she knew. She appreciated it. Sometimes a simple thank you, sometimes just a smile. But for me, those little things felt bigger than she probably realized. Somewhere along the way, I realized I wasn’t just admiring her anymore. I was rooting for her not just because she was amazing... but because she became someone my heart quietly chose to love. I was already quietly rooting for her with my whole heart. Then one day, it was like a door that suddenly slammed shut from a gust of wind. The conversations that once flowed so easily like a river, suddenly dried up. The space where her name used to appear on my screen became empty. And before I could even understand what was happening, I found myself standing outside a door I could no longer knock on. No goodbye. No last conversation. Just quiet indifference. I won’t lie or pretend, there are still moments I catch myself wondering how she’s doing. Wishing she’s okay. Wishing life is being kind to her the way she deserves. But even with that silence, I don’t regret anything. And if my role in her story was simply to genuinely admire her, believe in her, and love her quietly from afar... then I’m still grateful that, even for a while, my world got to have someone like her in it. -YearnerFinalBoss

So, I officially lost my cool today (6 Mar 26) at the Forestry waiting shed. ​If you’ve ever queued for a Forestry jeep after 4PM, you know the stakes. The time in between jeeps is erratic, the line is growing by the second, and the humidity (especially now na summer na) is testing your last nerve. My students and I had been waiting forever, joking about how we’d finally snap if someone dared to cut the line. Little did I know, I was foreshadowing my own villain origin story. ​ ​The jeep finally rolls up, but [REDACTED_NAME] stops it right in the middle of the queue—an irritating error to me (you'll know why). Well, to my absolute horror, two students (judging by the youthful audacity) who were at the back of the queue didn’t even glance at the line. They just bolted for the front passenger seat. This immediately triggered a "Hunger Games" or a "Zombie Apocalypse" response. Suddenly, everyone (except my students, who stayed disciplined) tried to abandoned the queue and seemingly about to swarm the jeep like it was the last helicopter out of a disaster zone. ​ ​I was so livid that my professional filter just... EVAPORATED. Before I could stop myself, I yelled out to the crowd: ​"Mga b*b* ba [REDACTED_NAME]?!" ​It sorta worked. Most people froze, suddenly remembering they were supposed to be functioning members of a civil society. But those two students in the front seat? They just sat there, laughing like they hadn't just spit on the concept of basic decency. I was shooting d*ggers at them, but apparently, they’re immune to public shame. ​It’s honestly frustrating. We like to talk about "nation-building" and "serving the people," but we can’t even handle the intellectual complexity of a single-file line. If our scholars ([REDACTED_NAME], para sa bayan?) and workers are this quick to ignore proper systems the moment things get slightly inconvenient, no wonder this country is in a constant state of chaos. Disrespect for the queue is just a gateway dr*g to larger forms of corruption. ​ ​So, was I wrong? Should I have just "embraced the chaos" and let the law of the jungle take over, since forestry naman yun? Or is it okay to occasionally remind people that being a "scholar" includes having the common sense to wait your turn? ​Because if "[REDACTED_NAME] ba [REDACTED_NAME]?!" is the wrong response to a total collapse of discipline, I don't think I want to be right anymore. I questioned worth, I questioned fate, Why hearts can open up too late. If loving her was meant to scar, Or teach me who I really are. It hurts to wonder what went wrong, Or why I held that hope so long. Yet through the quiet she left here, I’m learning how to face the fear.

To my dream girl, where my arms can't reach, my hearts still holds //YOU\\ Loving you from afar is one of the most beautiful experience I have felt. I will always root for you. Always always. There is a quiet warmth in my heart whenever I think of you. They say Distance makes the heart grow fond, it has a strange way of making love feel more intense, not because it weakens it, but because it reminds you every day how much you wish the person you love is right beside you. There are moments when I wish I could pause time and freeze the world just to be with you for a little while longer, for one more moment. To sit beside you, to hear your voice is so magnificent, that shared moment only between us. Sometimes the longing becomes so strong that I close my eyes and imagine what it would feel like to finally see you again. But even with the distance, there is still warmth in loving you. Because every thought of you brings comfort. Every memory reminds me that what I feel for you is real. Even when you’re not here, you are present in my dreams — in the way I smile when something reminds me of you, in the quiet moments when I whisper your name in my heart, in the hope that one day the miles between us become shorter distances. Missing you has taught me how deep love can go. It has shown me that love isn’t only about being together physically. Sometimes it’s about choosing someone even when they’re far away. It’s about carrying them in your heart through every day, through every moment of longing, through every night when all you want is to feel their presence. And even though I miss you more than words can explain, I hold on to the warmth of knowing that somewhere out there, the person my heart longs for exists. That alone makes the distance a little easier to bear. Because loving you, even from afar, is still one of the most precious feelings I have. I miss you, more than you ever know my dream girl. I love you.

On Loving Unconditionally and Smiling Genuinely I was twenty, drowning in the quiet chaos of my worst university semester, when we met. It was a season when I had stopped chasing the possibility of romantic love, having had zero experience with it anyway. I was carefree yet failing, floating through disappointed stares and existing at my absolute lowest. He met me there, in that wreckage—and perhaps, in his own way, I met him at his lowest, too. By the end of the semester, we were constantly talking. Before we left for our respective provinces, we agreed to have dinner—just the two of us—a small, private anchor before the distance of the break. By the end of the year, he asked if he could start courting me. I said yes, and we stepped into January feeling like a fresh beginning—no longer just two people talking, but two people officially finding their way to each other. I remember the chill of that first week in . We were back at the university, navigating paperwork and the nervous energy of new affection. After a trip to an amusement park—a short escape from our looming responsibilities—the shift happened. I don’t remember the rides or the noise, but I remember the choice. I was the one who reached out first. I was the one who initiated the holding of hands, letting the warmth of his palm confirm what my heart was just beginning to admit. On January 19th, I told him, "Yes," and we became official. He asked for permission to kiss me, a gesture so tender it set the tone for the years to follow. He became my MOON, the light that didn’t eliminate the dark but guided me through it. In his eyes, I had no flaws. He was my best friend, my protector, and the person I believed would carry me through anything. The Shadow and the Light But love, even unconditional love, has its darkness. There were days of silence where messages went unreturned, moments where I felt “too much,” and nights where someone cried their heart out while the other stayed quiet. It wasn’t perfect, but it held value. In our first year, I remember seeing a post he made. He said he would be a little happier if someone gifted him the book series A Song of Ice and Fire and Fire and Blood by [REDACTED_NAME]. I had no money then, only meager savings, but I spent weeks hunting for a pre-loved set I could afford. When I finally found one, it felt like more than a gift; it was a testament. To me, he deserved everything, and I wanted to be the one to provide the happiness he was looking for. The Distance and the Depletion Then the world slowed down. The pandemic forced us into a long-distance struggle, testing us in environments we weren’t prepared for. When the lockdowns lifted, I convinced my parents to let me move back near the university just to be near him. But life remained complicated; he chose not to see me most days because his sister was pregnant at the time. I completely understood, yet I still found myself begging for a presence I thought I had earned by moving closer. It felt like I was the only one trying to close the distance. It took a freak accident—a needle puncturing my finger while I was sewing and a subsequent surgery—for the universe to force us into the same room for longer than mere hours. For a while, the “softness” returned. At his sister’s suggestion, he stayed with me. We lived together, we studied together, and I finally began to pass the subjects that had once defeated me. My family fully embraced him, opening the doors of our home and our lives to him without reservation. We grew up within that shared space. We were twenty when we started; we were nearly thirty when we stopped. We stood by each other through final defenses and shared the triumph of a delayed graduation. But even with that success, I ended things before the date of our graduation ceremony arrived. The Hard Choice of Letting Go We broke up for the first time in , only to gravitate back together almost a year later. But by [REDACTED_NAME] , I realized that loving someone unconditionally can also mean becoming completely spent. It felt like I’d been carrying more than I could handle—emotionally, mentally, even silently. I tried to make things work, hoping the love I had would be enough to carry us through. But the truth is, I was tired. Our relationship was just dragging, and we weren’t growing as individuals anymore. I was staying not because I felt full, but because I was afraid of what breaking away would do—to him, to us, to everything we’d built. Ending things again felt cruel. As the decision took hold, I began to punish myself relentlessly; it felt as though being the villain in our story was sharply carved into my very being. I questioned how I could be the source of such wreckage for the person I loved most in the world, not once, but twice. I felt beyond redemption. Yet, looking back from , I still see the necessity of the pain. We had to grow. We couldn’t just settle into an environment that felt “comfortable” because we should be the ones who make the environment we stay in safe and comfortable for both of us—and we were not capable of doing exactly that in that moment. The Smile in the Frame Today, I look at the few photos of us I have left. I study my face—that specific, radiant smile. It was a genuine smile, the kind that only exists when you believe your heart is fully held. My smile today doesn’t look like that. I haven’t reached acceptance yet. There is still enough hope to make me want to stay, but enough pain to make me realize I must step back for his sake as much as my own. I have lost him, and in the process, I fear I have lost that version of my smile, too. I loved him without conditions at the beginning, and I am letting him go now with the hardest condition of all: the need for us both to finally, truly, grow up. And if ever God brings us back together, I hope we return as whole people—capable of honoring the weight, seeing the truth, and protecting the sanctity of love exactly the way it should be.

I hope you know that I remember what you said more than two years ago. We were supposed to be just friends, but we always knew it was something more. It was only ever addressed during alcohol induced moments. While you watched over me while I slept, I heard you ask, “Why didn’t we end up together?” Back then I wondered how you could say something so cruel. You were already talking to somebody else, after all. So instead of ruining the friendship, in a moment of clarity against all of my gin muddled thoughts, I told you, “We’re fine the way we are now.” I should have just pretended to sleep and let you count my breaths. In the morning, I pretended to forget. Now, two years later, in a completely different phase of our lives, I hope you know that I remembered what you said. But to nurture this distance, I hope you never know that I loved you then. - womenlosingwomen

What to do?! How to move on from a person who only talks to me when convenient to her?! We didn't even know each other that long, we met when we are graduating already but God knows how I tried to restrain myself. I made a promise to my parents that I will not be in a relationship until I graduated. I met her in a bio class. I never knew who she was back then, she's withdrawn and silent, keeps to herself always maybe talks to a seatmate about the topic. But you just find your eyes settling on her because she is the prettiest girl in the room, in the class, scratch that-- in the campus. Somehow we became group mates in the said class, so we got to know each other through group works then the rest is history. Fast forward to February we are talking about this coming activity and I look at her eyes and it happened — I fell in love. She is always avoidant when it comes to romantic topics, we go out but only as "friends" as that is how she views me ever since. Maybe it is her laugh, maybe it is her voice, but I could listen to that music. Maybe it is her softness, her small gestures toward me that made a lasting impression. Maybe it is her being amazing at almost everything because she works hard, keeps at it until she gets it right. I already knew my feelings are not reciprocated but I can tell there is something there. I confessed. I told her I like her, but I avoided using the word love or that I fell for her because she could withdraw from me even as friends. I wanted to know. I wanted answers. I was almost praying — I am atheist — she feels the same and half praying there could be something. I do not know anymore. Maybe it is my fault for falling but god knows every man in this world will fall for her if they know her. I buried myself with academics to forget about her, but given her impression on me I keep dreaming about her and me doing things together still as friends but I could feel the spark there. Until I wake up — she is not mine.
May meaning ba 'yung pag-add mo sa 'kin.

I've had the intention to pursue her before and was plotting on it but then she started seeing someone else a couple months back. I found out that they recently called it off, although they weren't actually together-together as in an official couple, I believe they dated with intention for months and that was the first guy she ever dated. She is a very private person and keeps to herself so it's hard to gauge exactly where her mind is at the moment. Apparently, she also liked me before she started seeing him so I guess mutual interest isn't really a problem here. I just don't wanna swoop in too early and scare her away, but I also don't wanna wait too long and lose my chance with her again. Honestly, I wouldn't mind becoming a rebound and trying to pursue someone who hasn't moved on yet, but I also think that she is too kind of a person to use someone like that. Please help a yearning isko out

I honestly didn't think that it's possible to gather up my confidence as an acne girlie, but seeing this one girl in my class literally changed my whole life and I hope she sees this message. For context, I've been admiring this girl in my class nung first day pa lang and I was too shy to even befriend her. First of all she styles her clothes sooo good, she always wear brown, wears make-up, and the only thing we have in common is acne. I always admired how she looks, never seen her unconfident— part of me thought it was because of wearing make-up but I was so wrong! I never tried to style my clothes or even wear make-up so my mindset was like that and I was never proven so wrong my whole life. I think a month has passed this 2nd sem and seeing her so consistent styling clothes and wearing make-up made me honestly give up on looking good with acne because I don't have the m-ntal and physical capacity to even just wake-up early and think of what I'm gonna wear and apply make-up. That mindset changed one day because this week, this girl showed up to class not wearing make-up and just some casual clothes and she still looks so goddamn good!!! I'm not saying that not wearing make-up = ugly to other people but that's what I thought for myself. She was also still confident speaking and not lowering her composure (like I do 😢) and it made me realize I'm not ugly because of my acne, I just need that confidence like she has. I know how hard it is to have acne and I think it didn't take her just a few hours to get that confidence, she worked hard to build that and I feel so sad that I didn't start building my own confidence sooner. If you're seeing this, I just want to let you know that I'm so so thankful that you made me realize everything I needed to reflect on about myself. I hope you continue being confident without thinking what others think and also for all acne girlies out there! soon I hope I can apply it to myself also.. - iskang acne girly

Initially, passion project [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] I realized that transit information around the campus would be a great help to first-timers and visitors. Unfortunately, kahit na yung jeepney talaga yung pinaka-nagmamakeup ng transport system natin, [REDACTED_NAME] sufficient navigational tools para malaman kung paano ito gamitin wisely, or how to get to where we want to go, and that’s why I designed these diagrams. I want to turn these diagrams into permanent, weather-proof fixtures across the campus. There is a warmth in [CALABARZON] that is hard to explain, but you feel it instantly. You feel so safe here. It is a rare and beautiful thing that we will carry with us forever. But life can also be rigid. It is not always soft. It is not always easy. And because life is hard, none of us should have to fight it alone. You carry so much weight on your shoulders, but you do not ask for help. You keep it inside. But if life was easy, there would be no need for teachers. There would be no need for mentors or even helpers. We have them because we are not meant to survive this on our own. Sometimes, there are people standing right next to you who want to help. They are ready. But because you don't ask, they don't know. That is what hurts the most. We miss the chance to lift each other up just because of silence. You are special. You deserve to be graced. You deserve kindness and support. Do not be afraid to let people in. I admire you all so much. I hope you all enjoy the Feb Fair. Make beautiful memories. From ❤️

If you are reading this, I hope my life is finally at peace. I have decided to finally finish it (or not). I’m a graduate student, and I messed up. One mistake in class, that’s all it took. In a place where excellence is expected and hesitation feels like weakness, there’s no room for mistakes. That’s how it felt. I could see it in their faces. The judgment felt real enough. Graduate school is not for the weak. UP is not for the weak. And suddenly I was convinced I didn’t belong here. Like I had slipped through some crack in the system and everyone had finally noticed. An impostor exposed. Years of hard work reduced to a single misstep. It’s terrifying how quickly confidence can collapse. How one moment can start pulling at every thread you used to hold yourself together. Shame has a way of warping the mirror; you look at yourself and see someone smaller, lesser, a fraud! My chest feels heavy with it. The kind of heavy that doesn’t lift with sleep. So if this reaches you, I hope I had the courage to end it.

The Marginalized Friend Ansakit pala noh, [REDACTED_NAME] second thought na friend. [REDACTED_NAME] actually, na I don't fit as well with the group, but even then it just h*rts. You see your friends walking away from you to go somewhere, and you don't even get an invite. When things get rough between you and someone from the said group, you don't even get the chance to explain your side. It's just awkward silence; ang motto nila pagdating sayo "time heals", but at the end of the day, you'd still be the first one to reach out. Or maybe, I'm misinterpreting everything. Maybe I'm just too d*mb that I thought I belonged in the first place. Or maybe I just care too much and love too much? I think about them many times, and perhaps, I'm too invested into what we have as friends. But damn, can I even blame myself? I've always felt like a commodity -- a convenience, someone easy to talk with, not much drama, agreeable, generous, and what not. I'm one of those, you can just leave him alone, resilient friend, and you can always come back to him; and as much as I agree with that, I also want to feel loved and looked after at. I started to believe that I am no longer seen as such, but I think I still am. Nature doesn't reward the good, the kind -- these are constructs after all. But damn, at least reassure me that I belong, that I may still reach out. Am I too kind? Am I too old? Am I too agreeable; should I disagree more? Why does it feel like cruel and evil people have better friendships than I do? This s-cks man.

Im thirteen again. Alone, I walk the pathway, of my dreams realized. Did I push everyone out again? Am i stuck in the same hole i took years climbing out of? Why can't I make as meaningful connection as everybody else? Why do i always end up alone? Why am I always left roaming alone? Do they even remember my name? Was I just a mist passing through their vast life? Did they not see my face? My image contorted in their own memories. My name spoken with such bland disregard. I'm a companion of no one but my own inabilities. I can only watch as they leave. I can't find my voice, stunned that they have not remembered me. Maybe its just my nature, I am not a permanent thing in their lives. Who have made me? Who is my frankenstein? So I can drag him and take his heart out. Put it in me and for once believe that I am to be remembered. I am desolate, alone with my own thoughts. So in this life, do i surpress? Be content living a life with no trace? Surviving with no hearts touched? No friends to call my own, no body to hold when I wither. Do i lose because i cant even utter a word infront of people i used to know? And do i break because I look for them everywhere i go? -gthm

For this, I developed EZRecord, a mobile application that allows informal and micro businesses to easily accept and record transactions done via E-Payment applications such as GCash. I leveraged computer vision and object character recognition in order to automatically locate payment fields and extract them as text, allowing for streamlined recording of E-Payments. Various features of the application include: - Auto-scan of E-Payment Records - Inventory Management - Transactions Management - Statistics - Transactions Export The application does not need internet access in order to work. to L, 🐼 we used to believe love was a place we could return to, like no matter how far we drifted, there would always be a light on in the kitchen and your voice saying, come back, it’s okay. before, every problem felt smaller because it had two of us holding it. we didn’t even need answers— we’d just sit in silence, knees touching, breathing the same air, and somehow that was enough to make the world behave. now the silence is louder. now it doesn’t know my name. i keep asking the night if i’m supposed to wait for you to find your way back, or if loving you means learning how to walk forward alone. i don’t know if there’s someone else filling the space where my hands used to be, or if you’re staring at the same ceiling, wondering when everything started to slip. i know i made mistakes— the kind you don’t see until they’re already echoes. i know i said the wrong things when i was just scared of losing you. i wasn’t trying to break us. i was trying to hold on too tightly. tell me how people navigate life after losing the one they planned it with. how do i make decisions when you were always the answer? how do i choose a future when every version i wanted had your name in it? if there’s still a chance— even a fragile, barely-breathing one— to start over, slower this time, softer, i would meet you there with everything i’ve learned. but if you’re already gone, if this love is meant to live only in memory, please know this: i loved you in the quiet, in the chaos, in the moments that didn’t make it into photographs. and even now, confused and breaking, a part of me still believes we were once something worth fighting for. -updownleftright

In reply to . While the concern about microplastics and waste is valid, placing the primary responsibility on small student retailers oversimplifies the issue. Student organizations during events like FebFair operate within real constraints of affordability, availability, and environmental conditions such as muddy grounds and rain. Shoe covers address a practical need for mobility and safety, not mere convenience. I personally chose to buy shoe covers because I only have one pair of shoes, and if those get muddy, I would need to spend at least ₱400 for professional cleaning—an expense that is not feasible for many students. In this context, shoe covers are a preventive and cost-effective option. Environmental responsibility is shared, not unilateral. If disposable products are being sold, accountability must also extend to event organizers who manage waste systems, consumers who are responsible for proper disposal, and institutions that are expected to provide accessible sustainable alternatives and supporting infrastructure. Expecting student sellers to resolve systemic waste issues without institutional backing shifts responsibility downward and distracts from addressing the root causes. When discussing wasted resources, it is also important to consider trade-offs. Professional shoe cleaning involves water, detergents, electricity, transportation, and packaging, all of which carry environmental costs. Preventive measures, even if temporary, can reduce repeated resource use over time, particularly for students who do not have the privilege of multiple footwear options. Ecological literacy should therefore push for better systems—such as proper waste management, collection mechanisms, and clearer sustainability guidelines from organizers—rather than singling out student initiatives responding to immediate and real conditions. Sustainability works best when solutions are collective, practical, and supported at all levels. True ecological literacy recognizes that sustainability must be accessible, practical, and inclusive. Critiquing student sellers and buyers without addressing structural limitations risks turning environmentalism into a privilege—something only those with financial flexibility can afford.

Been with this guy for 2 years and broke up last November because he changed. We started when I was still living with a relative that abused me and my main coping mechanism was by shutting off my emotions, which also led me to become em*tionally unavailable for him. That s how our relationship started. It changed once I got out, I did as he asked to become softer, because I was cold, and after that, I was honestly ready to do everything for him because I was so grateful about staying with me through it. So that's what I did, I dedicated everything to him but I balanced it out with my studies. Moving out felt like it was finally my time to experience everything I couldn't experience in my college life, just focusing on studying, having a love life that supports me, and trying out other new experiences. But I didn't notice he's already changed but I just forgave everything. He associated everything that had to do with me as a bother, belittled my attempts at trying to grow, was looking at other girls whenever we fought to get his mind off me, and marked everything else as more important. I tried as much as I could to forgive him every single time until I had nothing in me left that even I didn't leave any for myself. We agreed to go our separate ways. I didn't contact him once after splitting but at the start of this sem, he messaged me about an injury he got and I couldn't help it. I helped treated his wounds because I honestly still care about him. But I didn't plan on going back. He opened up about how he missed me so much and that he spent days being miserable and crying here and there. I didn't have that experience during our split because I had finally experienced only having to think of myself for myself and it made me realize I wasn't insecure, I didn't have anx**ty, and I slept well. Somehow we kinda got back together but I couldn't help but come back to the wounds from the past because none of it had been resolved. None of it had been talked about properly. Every time I tried to talk about it, I was shut down immediately by a rebuttal, a defense, or him telling me he's tired. Now I'm miserable and I keep crying about it because he keeps on showing me he doesn't care about me anymore but I'm so confused because he also shows me other things and I just want clarity, I want him to acknowledge everything he did that hurt me because every time I confront him about it, he keeps defending himself. I wish he'd just apologize, not even like how I did to him but just acknowledge what he did h*rt me. Now I don't know what to do because I really wanted this sem and my remaining time here to be about growth and peace. But I'm just really overwhelmed and miserable with everything I feel about all this again.

Is it bad to feel upset when your partner gives you gifts that they want instead of what you actually want? Don’t get me wrong, I appreciate the effort and the fact that they even thought of giving something. But sometimes it feels like the gift is more about them than about me. Like, I’ve mentioned things I genuinely like or need, and yet I still receive things that clearly match their taste or interests. I feel guilty for being disappointed but at the same time, isn’t the point of gift-giving to make the other person feel seen and understood? Or am I just being ungrateful and overthinking this? Would love to hear thoughts because I don’t know if this is a red flag, a communication issue, or just a me problem.

Hi... I don’t really know how to start this, but I want to take this chance to finally confess what I’ve been feeling. This isn’t the usual kind of confession, because the person I like isn’t just anyone—he was my professor. Yes, my professor. He was new at [CALABARZON] back then, coming from a different home unit, and he started a year before I graduated. I first had him as my professor when he was still new. He was incredibly kind, and honestly, I didn’t even see his full face at the time because he always wore a face mask. Still, there was something about his presence that stood out. He had this gentleness that affected me in a way I couldn’t explain. His voice was so calming whenever he spoke, and even something as simple as his perfectly wavy hair made him memorable. I wasn’t sure what I was feeling whether it was just a crush or pure inspiration but I knew it was something I wanted to keep. The following semester, he became my professor again, this time in around three of my subjects. I would sometimes fall asleep during his last class of the day, partly because my schedule was exhausting, but also because his voice was so calming. During that time, I was often absent due to health issues. Despite that, he never made me feel uncomfortable about it. He helped me catch up, allowed me to take missed requirements, and showed so much consideration. He never questioned my absences—he simply understood. There was even one activity I completely missed, and he personally guided me so I could still complete it. There is this one time where I was shocked that he noticed that I was in that room the entire day and told me that “Kaya ka [REDACTED_NAME] inaantok sa class [REDACTED_NAME] araw ka pala dito.” That made me feel noticed. There was also a conference we both attended. Maybe because I was excited, I greeted him with an energy I had never shown before. Throughout the conference, I would catch small glimpses of him every day, exchange brief conversations, and quietly appreciate those moments. In my last semester, I thought I would never see him again. Then one day, as I was entering my classroom, I saw him coming out of his office. We passed each other in silence, and I thought that would be the last time. But during my defense, I was shocked to see him as one of my panelists. I gave my best that day maybe because I wanted to impress him. The entire time, I could barely look at him while I was speaking. It felt like the longest moment of my life. And that was the last time I saw him. You may never read this, and maybe this confession will always remain unspoken. But I want to thank you for being my inspiration in ways you’ll never know. Every time I saw you, I couldn’t help but smile. I know your messages to me were always purely academic, and I understood that. Still, I couldn’t help feeling butterflies whenever you reached out, even if it was just to ask something related to class. I hope that someday, I get to see you again and maybe, if I ever find the courage, I’ll finally thank you in person. iska202xxx

[REDACTED_NAME]'s week will be here soon; hence I made a short story in the spirit of it. I am not a writer, nor have I written anything similar to this recently, so I sincerely apologize for any errors. Please enjoy One day, [REDACTED_NAME] and I were walking along the roads of Mondstadt, when he suddenly knelt down on one knee and proposed to me. "[REDACTED_NAME], I want to be yours for the rest of my life. Will you take my hand in marriage?" he spoke. Of course, since it's Varka, I instantly accepted his proposal. "Yes, my dearest [REDACTED_NAME]. I will be yours, and you will be mine 'til death do us part. I love you so much!" I replied. And then, we walked hand-in-hand around Mondstadt, announcing and celebrating our engagement. As time passed on, we made arrangements and preparations for our wedding day. We checked out flower shops, saved up millions of [REDACTED_NAME] and fates, and picked out our wedding locations together. One day, while we were rewriting the limits of love underneath the tree in Windrise, the skies became overcast with red clouds. And before we knew it, the Heavenly Principles came down from the heavens. "We have been awakened and notified of your wrongdoings, Descender. You have committed the sin of forming a romantic bond with a Teyvatian human, despite of your alien origins. Hence, you shall be punished greatly for it. Please behave accordingly and do not repeat this offense. This is the first warning" they echoed from the sky. Before I could take out my sword, they hold out their arm in the direction of my Varka. As soon as they moved their hand, I witnessed every being of Varka morphed into something no longer human. I looked away for a moment, as I could not bear to see his disfigurement. I quickly scanned the area, in hopes that anyone else could be witnessing this, but as it turns out, everything aside from me, the Heavenly Principles, and Varka were frozen in time. After what felt like a long time, the Heavenly Principles disappeared, and the skies turned back to normal. Once my gaze returned to the love of my life, I could no longer recognize the sight before me. The form that was supposed to be my fiancé opened its mouth: "Ma-a-a-a" it bleated. I could not believe the cruel punishment the heavens bestowed upon me. [REDACTED_NAME], the man who proposed to me weeks ago, reduced to a mere mammal. Despite this, I attempted to speak to it. "Varka, are you okay? It's me, [REDACTED_NAME]." I choked out as I held back my tears. "Ma-a-a" it spoke before walking off to a distance. I tried to give chase, but as soon as it heard me running, it also started to run. Then, I made a horrifying realization: it does not recognize me at all, nor does it have the rationality to remember the time we spent together. It took a long while to catch up to my "Varka", who only stopped because it fell into the river near the Mondstadt bridge. In that moment, I just broke down. Because then, I knew, My goat is washed.

I did not expect graduate school at [CALABARZON] to feel this heavy. I came in with quiet hope. Not confidence, just the belief that hard work would be enough. I told myself I belonged here because I earned the slot, because I loved the field, because I was willing to stay up late and read one more paper. Then the grades came. Not failures, just low. Numbers that carried weight. Mediocre. That word hurt more than I thought it would. Each semester ended the same way, not with shock, just a steady disappointment. I checked my grades alone. No noise. Just me and the screen. I stared longer than needed, as if waiting might change something. It never did. I closed the tab and felt small in a place that once felt exciting. The classes were hard, but the harder part was watching myself grow quiet. I stopped raising my hand. I stopped asking questions unless I was sure they were safe. Others spoke with ease about papers I struggled through. I did the work. I submitted on time. Still, the feedback stayed flat. Not harsh, not kind. Just enough to remind me I was average here. I began to doubt myself. Maybe I am not smart enough. Maybe I was wrong to be here. Those thoughts came late at night, when there was nothing to distract me. From the outside, everything looked fine. I was still enrolled. Still passing. People said I was doing okay. I smiled, because explaining felt harder than staying silent. There were days I wanted to quit, not because I h*ted the work, but because I was tired of feeling behind. But I stayed. Not out of strength, just stubbornness. I kept showing up. I learned that staying does not always look brave. Sometimes it is quiet, dull, and lonely.

Regarding . “I am planning to actually recive BUKLOD-[CALABARZON].” Hey kid. While i so admire your courage to revive the party but as an alumnus of the org qI advise you not to do so. Why? The org and the party has made a mark in [CALABARZON]'s colorful history of campus politics. It has already ran its course. The political discourse in our country has significantly changed. Also, the members of the party and the organization have moved on to pursue their lives beyond the sacred grounds of [CALABARZON]. Some pursued professional growth, some pursued quieter lives and some continued in different capacities. Your challenge being the new generation of student-leaders eager to enter campus politics is to how to make yourself effective leaders in this ever changing volatile landscape. Branding will not define your path a student leaders but your impact will define it. Ask yourselves the purpose and capabilities of you running and will this be the best part for you 10 to 20 years from now. I am proud being part of history that was BUKLOD [CALABARZON] but it is now your time to create your own destiny beyond political party names. Its how would you spark change from within.

I want to share some excerpts from this piece I wrote after I broke up with her: Maybe we could work, I kept thinking to myself. We loved each other after all, why wouldn’t it work? However, we were not in a movie. Not a k-drama. Not a telenovela. We are in life. Sometimes, it does feel like we are oceans apart. We live in two worlds. I am unfamiliar with your comforts and you are unfamiliar with my struggles. I eat canned goods and think they are food; you always tell me to add veggies and fruits to my diet. We are aliens in each other’s worlds. You are [REDACTED_NAME] and I am Neo; I can already fly while you are still discovering the secrets of the world you live in. I am tired and you are too. We have been for quite some time, I think. We both just didn’t know how to tell each other, and that’s alright. I understand you. I do not blame you for this. I do not blame myself for this. It’s okay. Maybe we are just Rio and [REDACTED_NAME]. Your walls were high up and I didnt know what to do anymore. It’s okay, [REDACTED_NAME]. It’s okay. It hurts me so much to say goodbye to you for the last time. It hurts to see you staring at nothingness while we were on our way home. It hurts to see you hurt because of this breakup. It hurts me every time I remember the look on your face while we were in the jeep, and I wanted so much to take back my decision. It hurts so much, I know, but I knew that we had to do it. That we have to break up because... For the months that we shared, I saw and heard you. I started listening to you. But, alas, you forgot. You forgot to do the same to me. I’ll be here and you’ll be there, but we will never meet again. We will remain in our separate worlds. We are [REDACTED_NAME] and Rio. This is where our story ends. Goodbye and thank you for the memories and lessons. I love you. Letting go, That boy who turned out to be a girl from [NCR].

I walked back home today drenched with sadness. The night sky was clear and beautiful. The brightest bodies reminded me of your smile, and their distance reminded me of how far I am from reaching them. I bought alcohol to quiet my soul. I sat in awe of their beauty, but the thought of ever reaching them humbled my feeble capacity as a human being. I had to delude myself to keep the tears from spilling. “Perhaps you’re the moon—bright, beautiful, perfectly composed. It takes a great deal of effort to reach you, but it isn’t impossible. Perhaps you’re Jupiter—larger, more radiant, and farther still. It would take generational talent to stand beside you.” These were lies. Because you’re the brightest thing I’ve ever seen, and celestial bodies other than stars cannot quite encapsulate you. A small light beside the brightest star only disappears. The brightest stars need an equally bright star to make something meaningful. It is self-deprecating to call myself the weakest light, but even knowing my potential, my radiance pales beside yours. And yet I’m stuck in a limbo. I’m drawn to you like a body tidally locked to something larger and condemned to circle, to watch, to want, to never quite arrive. I have to live with the pain of appreciating you from afar while aching to be closer. The only way this changes is if you turn your gaze toward me, and miraculously choose, somehow, to close the distance.

To the man I once liked, and who is now a complete stranger—yes, you. I tried to restrain my feelings because you were in a relationship back then, and I didn’t want to ruin what you had with your partner. I kept my distance, even though we were together almost every day. We used to sit on one of the benches in Freedom Park, laughing at the smallest things and talking about anything and everything. We never ran out of stories. Time always passed so quickly when I was with you. I liked you back then, but I knew you belonged to someone else, so I chose not to speak about my feelings. I let things be. I waited, quietly wondering if you would ever love me enough to choose me—but you didn’t. When she found out about our closeness, we decided to go our separate ways. I never told you how I truly felt. A few months later, you messaged me, saying you wished you had chosen me instead. I still remember those exact words. But I chose not to reply. I chose not to speak. I chose silence—because I knew you were still with her. Looking back now, and seeing where we both are, I know I made the right choice. Some feelings are meant to be felt, not confessed. Some stories are meant to end quietly.
